questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a NH real estate salesperson license?
questions.explanation: New Hampshire requires salesperson applicants to be at least 18 years of age. This is consistent with the legal age to enter into binding contracts in the state.

questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a NH salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: NH requires 40 hours of pre-license education for salesperson candidates. Of these, a minimum of 32 hours must be completed in a classroom setting, with a maximum of 8 hours allowed via distance education.

questions.question_text: A candidate wants to complete all 40 pre-license hours online. Is this permitted in NH?
questions.correct_answer: No, at least 32 hours must be classroom
distractors: Yes, all hours can be online
No, all 40 hours must be classroom
questions.explanation: NH limits distance education to a maximum of 8 hours of the 40-hour pre-license requirement. At least 32 hours must be completed in a traditional classroom setting to ensure adequate hands-on instruction.

questions.question_text: Within what time frame must a candidate complete pre-license education before taking the state exam?
questions.explanation: NH requires that the 40-hour pre-license course be completed within 6 months of sitting for the licensing exam. Candidates who exceed this window must retake the course before attempting the exam.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed pre-license education 8 months ago and now wants to take the exam. What must the candidate do?
questions.explanation: Since pre-license education must be completed within 6 months of the exam, a candidate whose course was completed 8 months prior has exceeded the validity window and must retake the entire 40-hour pre-license course.

questions.question_text: What criminal background check is required for NH licensure?
questions.explanation: NH requires a criminal records check through the NH State Police as a condition of licensure. The results of this check are valid for 6 months from the date of issuance.

questions.question_text: A candidate obtained a criminal records check 7 months ago. Can she use this for her license application?
questions.correct_answer: No, the check is only valid for 6 months
distractors: Yes, it is valid for 12 months
Yes, there is no expiration date
No, she needs a federal check instead
questions.explanation: The NH State Police criminal records check is valid for only 6 months. After that period, the applicant must obtain a new records check to submit with the license application.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the national portion of the NH exam but fails the state portion. What happens next?
questions.correct_answer: The candidate must retake the state portion
distractors: The candidate receives a full license
Both portions must be retaken together
questions.explanation: The NH licensing exam is split — candidates must pass both the national and state portions independently at 70%. If only one portion is failed, the candidate retakes only that portion within the allowed timeframe.

questions.question_text: A candidate scores 75% on the national portion but 65% on the state portion. What is the result?
distractors: Only the state portion must be retaken
questions.explanation: Candidates must achieve at least 70% on each portion of the exam independently. Although the candidate passed the national portion (75%), the 65% on the state portion is below the 70% threshold, resulting in exam failure.

questions.question_text: A candidate has failed the NH licensing exam multiple times. What does NH require before allowing additional attempts?
questions.correct_answer: Retake the pre-license course after repeated failures
distractors: There is no limit on exam attempts in NH
The candidate must wait one year to retake
The candidate must obtain a waiver from OPLC
questions.explanation: NH requires candidates who repeatedly fail the licensing exam to retake the entire pre-license education course before making additional attempts. This ensures candidates receive updated instruction to address knowledge gaps.

questions.question_text: What is the NH real estate license renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: New Hampshire real estate licenses are renewed on a biennial (every two years) basis. The renewal fee is $90, and licensees must complete 15 hours of continuing education during each renewal period.

questions.question_text: How many continuing education hours are required per renewal period in NH?
questions.explanation: NH licensees must complete 15 hours of continuing education each biennial renewal period. This includes 3 hours of mandatory core topics and 12 hours of elective courses.

questions.question_text: A licensee completes 12 elective CE hours but skips the core requirement. Can the license be renewed?
questions.explanation: The 15-hour CE requirement includes 3 mandatory core hours that cannot be replaced by elective hours. Both the 3 core hours and 12 elective hours must be completed for a valid renewal.

questions.question_text: What additional CE requirement applies to a licensee renewing for the first time?
questions.explanation: First-time renewals in NH require completion of post-licensing topics in addition to the standard CE requirements. This ensures new licensees receive supplemental education during their initial practice period.

questions.question_text: A licensed broker from Vermont wants to practice in NH. Under HB 594, what reciprocity does NH offer?
questions.explanation: HB 594 established universal reciprocity, allowing NH to recognize real estate licenses from all 50 states. This means a licensed broker from any state, including Vermont, can obtain a NH license through the reciprocity process.
